Ok, a lot of people have been asking this. You need any oculus link cable, a pc, and a vrm model. You can make a vrm model in Vroid studio. You don’t need a vr ready pc to do this. Connect the headset and pc with the cable. Go into the quest drive and go to the Play Animaker file. Go to models and copy and paste your .vrm model. RENAME IT “My Character”. Or else it won’t work. Go into the game and import model. Boom shot got it.
How do i import my own model? it only ever imports the My Charactar.vrm model.

So easy to create character animation in VR and I always wanted to make my own anime!

Can't wait for the full version!

reviews.review_title: Amazing very good and works very well
reviews.review_description: Works insanely well for what its meant for but in the full release do you plan on adding more avatars or an avatar customization type thing?

reviews.review_title: a fantastic app，the future of mmd
reviews.review_description:
after using this app for a few minutes，I've seen the future of mmd producing. The biggest  
 adventage that vr has comparing to traditional 2d monitor, is that using vr to operate the avatars to pose the correct action and add them to the keyframe. In vr you can just use your 6dof controllers to drag the avatar's body to the right place, just like teaching people how to dance in the real world. 
But in 2d monitor,you have to use keyboard to adjust the angle of their joints, or adjust ik bones, which make it a really difficult and time consuming jod to do.
Although the function of this app is still too few and imperfect, I still believe in the future of vr anime maker apps.

reviews.review_title: So cool and so much potential
